title: lophira alata var. procera
intro: Lophira alata var. procera, also known as the African walnut or white African walnut, is a large evergreen tree native to tropical Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Lophira alata var. procera include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as acting as an antiseptic and antifungal agent. In herbal preparations, the bark and seeds of the tree are typically used to make decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
